data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows
data.description:
On February 14, significant inflows were observed in U.S. Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs. According to BlockBeats, the net inflow for Bitcoin spot ETFs reached $15.1 million. Among the funds, BlackRock's IBIT saw an outflow of $9.4 million, while Fidelity's FBTC experienced an inflow of $12 million. VanEck's HODL and WisdomTree's BTCW recorded inflows of $1.9 million and $3.6 million, respectively. Grayscale's mini BTC fund also saw an inflow of $7 million.

In the Ethereum spot ETF market, there was a net inflow of $10.2 million. BlackRock's ETHA faced an outflow of $9.3 million, whereas Fidelity's FETH had an inflow of $2 million. VanEck's ETHV and Grayscale's mini ETH fund recorded inflows of $3 million and $14.5 million, respectively.

data.title: Trader Shows Bullish Sentiment with $3.97 Million NVIDIA Position
data.description: A newly established wallet has deposited 2 million USDC into Hyperliquid to initiate a 2x leveraged long position on NVIDIA (NVDA) stock. According to NS3.AI, the position is currently valued at approximately $3.97 million, comprising 21,687 NVDA tokens. This action indicates a strong bullish sentiment from the trader utilizing the Hyperliquid platform.

data.title: New Wallet Leverages NVDA Stock with Significant USDC Deposit
data.description: A newly established wallet has deposited 2 million USDC into Hyperliquid, leveraging NVDA stock with a 2x margin. According to Odaily, the wallet currently holds 21,687 xyz:NVDA, valued at $3.97 million.

data.title: Amazon's Potential Market Cap Growth Sparks Discussion
data.description: Crypto KOL IncomeSharks posted on X about the potential growth of Amazon's market capitalization. The discussion highlights the possibility of Amazon reaching a market cap of 5 trillion, which would make it the largest stock in history. This speculation comes in the context of the U.S. stock market's total value, which is approximately 69 trillion. The conversation reflects the significant impact such a milestone could have on the financial landscape.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $15.2029 million yesterday, with Fidelity FBTC leading the way with a net inflow of $11.9851 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $15.2029 million yesterday (February 13th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net inflow of $11.9851 million, bringing FBTC's historical total net inflow to $10.982 billion. This was followed by the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF (BTC), with a net inflow of $6.9947 million, bringing BTC's historical total net inflow to $2.05 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a net outflow of $9.3619 million, bringing IBIT's historical total net inflow to $61.606 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $87.038 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.33%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $54.329 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $10.2647 million yesterday, with Grayscale's ETH holdings leading the way with a net inflow of $14.5088 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $10.2647 million yesterday (February 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $14.5088 million, bringing ETH's historical total net inflow to $1.709 billion. This was followed by the VanEck ETF (ETHV), with a net inflow of $2.9954 million, bringing ETHV's historical total net inflow to $175 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $9.2802 million, bringing ETHA's historical total net inflow to $11.977 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $11.719 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.75%, and a cumulative net inflow of $11.648 billion.

data.title: Chinese Skiers Favor Domestic Resorts Over Japanese Destinations
data.description: Chinese skiers are increasingly choosing local resorts over popular Japanese destinations due to escalating political tensions between China and Japan. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this shift in preference among Chinese tourists. The change comes as diplomatic relations between the two nations have become strained, influencing travel decisions. As a result, domestic ski resorts in China are experiencing a surge in popularity, offering an alternative for winter sports enthusiasts. This trend reflects broader geopolitical dynamics impacting tourism and travel choices.

data.title: Multicoin Capital Supports Strategic Proposal by Aave Labs
data.description:
Multicoin Capital's investment team leader, Vishal Kankani, has expressed support for Aave Labs' proposal, according to Foresight News. As a significant holder of AAVE, Multicoin Capital views the proposal to redirect 10% of Aave's product revenue to the DAO as a strategic shift that could enhance Aave's token economic model. The company believes that if implemented correctly, this proposal could set a significant precedent for the entire DeFi sector.

The proposal involves the allocation of approximately $25 million in stablecoins, 75,000 AAVE tokens valued at around $9 million, and additional milestone rewards. This represents an economic exposure of over $40 million to $50 million, accounting for 25% to 30% of the total funds. Given the scale and pioneering nature of this funding request, Multicoin Capital suggests that more detailed financial and underwriting data should be provided to support the request's magnitude.

data.title: Unknown USDC-OCA Liquidity Pool on BSC Chain Attacked, Resulting in Significant Loss
data.description:
An unknown USDC-OCA liquidity pool on the BSC chain was attacked, leading to the extraction of approximately 422,000 USDC. According to PANews, the attacker exploited a vulnerability in the deflationary sellOCA() logic of the OCA token, which allowed them to artificially inflate the token price by removing an equivalent amount of OCA from the pool during each swap.

The attack was executed through three transactions: the first carried out the attack operation, while the latter two were primarily used to pay additional bribes to block builders. The attacker paid a total of approximately 43 BNB and 69 BNB to 48club-puissant-builder, ultimately profiting an estimated $340,000. Another transaction within the same block failed at position 52, suspected to have been front-run by the attacker.

data.title: U.S. Department of Homeland Security Faces Shutdown Over Funding Dispute
data.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security has been forced to shut down due to a funding deadlock. Bloomberg posted on X that the impasse arose following widespread opposition to U.S. President Donald Trump's stringent immigration raids. The shutdown highlights the ongoing tensions and disagreements over immigration policies within the government. The funding stalemate has left the department unable to operate, impacting its various functions and responsibilities. This development underscores the challenges faced by the administration in balancing security measures with public and political opposition.

data.title: A new Hyperliquid address has a 2x leveraged long position in Nvidia stock, with a position value of $3.97 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a newly created wallet deposited 2 million USDC into Hyperliquid 11 hours ago to go long on NVDA stock with 2x leverage. This address currently holds 21,687 xyz:NVDA, worth $3.97 million.

data.title: Two whales have been continuously accumulating H tokens on the blockchain, totaling nearly 74.4 million.
data.description: According to on-chain data tracking, from November 23, 2025 to February 14, 2026, two whale addresses continuously withdrew AI identity verification Humanity Protocol tokens (H) from centralized exchanges to on-chain addresses. The funds primarily originated from exchanges such as Bybit, Gate, and KuCoin, and were transferred to concentrated on-chain holding addresses, exhibiting characteristics of continuous accumulation and concentrated holding. Address 0x9184…6F42 withdrew a total of 23,149,471 H tokens, nearly representing its current total holdings; address 0xbaab…7ab2 withdrew a total of 51,253,804 H tokens, also accounting for the vast majority of its holdings.

data.title: Valentine’s Day Romance Scams: US Prosecutors Warn on Crypto Risks
data.description:
U. S. prosecutors have warned of Valentine’s Day romance scams using crypto after cases cost victims millions.
source: https://cryptopotato.com/valentines-day-romance-scams-us-prosecutors-warn-on-crypto-risks/

data.title: Concerns Raised Over Crypto Influencer's Expertise
data.description: Crypto KOL 陈剑Jason posted on X about a female influencer who frequently shares photos with celebrities and discusses success strategies. Recently, she claimed to have been invited by the city government to speak about cryptocurrency. A few days ago, she posted a photo of herself working late at a luxurious office desk, surrounded by draft papers filled with mathematical formulas, stating her intention to focus on more valuable pursuits. While her post was motivational, 陈剑Jason questioned the significance of solving a high school math problem in this context. He expressed no offense but raised concerns about her presenting herself as an expert to the public, especially given the existing skepticism towards cryptocurrency by the mainland government.

data.title: PolyBeats Releases Report on Prediction Markets Amid Russia-Ukraine Conflict
data.description:
On February 14, PolyBeats, a research brand under BlockBeats, released an analysis report on prediction markets related to the Russia-Ukraine conflict. According to BlockBeats, the report examines 79 events from the 'Russia-Ukraine Occupation' series on the Polymarket platform, analyzing data from 23,316 unique blockchain addresses and over 3.09 million transaction records. Through dual models focusing on behavior and capital concentration, the report identifies suspicious accounts with highly concentrated transactions, unusually stable win rates (80%-93%), and profits significantly deviating from typical retail characteristics.

The current prediction markets have evolved beyond simple betting tools, serving as a bridge between blockchain data and news discovery. The report aims to demonstrate how prediction markets can capture real geopolitical signals through abnormal on-chain trading behavior. PolyBeats applies this methodology for real-time monitoring and has successfully validated it across multiple domains.

The full report is available on the BlockBeats website and the PolyBeats public account 'PolyBeats Truth Platform.'

data.title: Hong Kong Residents Test Boundaries of Expression Under National Security Law
data.description: Following the sentencing of media tycoon Jimmy Lai to 20 years in prison, Hong Kong residents are examining the constraints on freedom of expression imposed by the territory's national security law. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing concerns among citizens about the implications of the law on their rights and freedoms. The national security law, which was enacted to curb dissent, has led to increased scrutiny and self-censorship among the population. As residents navigate these new legal boundaries, the impact on Hong Kong's social and political landscape remains a topic of significant discussion.

data.title: Stanford Professor Proposes Enhancements for Political Prediction Markets
data.description:
Stanford Business School Professor and advisor to a16z and Meta, Andy Hall, has highlighted issues within political prediction markets. According to Odaily, Hall's team developed a new dataset focusing on political prediction markets, liquidity, and settlement rules. Their research revealed that the majority of political contracts in prediction markets lack activity, with only 1.3% having sufficient liquidity. Additionally, platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket rarely list identical contracts with the same rules, leading to further liquidity fragmentation.

Hall proposed four improvements: first, listing contracts on core issues and collaborating with independent bodies to define markets of social interest; second, paying market makers to inject initial liquidity into political markets; third, introducing AI agents to trade in areas without human participation to generate necessary price references; and fourth, establishing unified definitions and settlement rules across platforms. Hall believes these measures will attract traders looking to hedge political risks, transforming prediction markets into truth machines needed by society.

data.title: X to Enhance Detection of Automated Behavior and Spam
data.description: X Product Lead and Solana Advisor Nikita Bier announced that X is set to introduce more mechanisms to detect automated behavior and spam. According to ChainCatcher, the company plans to continuously strengthen these measures. Accounts identified by the system as not being operated manually, including those used for technical testing or experiments, may face suspension along with their associated accounts.

data.title: U.S. Senators Urge Withdrawal of Social Media History Proposal for Visa-Free Visitors
data.description: Two Democratic U.S. Senators have called on the Trump administration to abandon a proposal requiring visitors from visa-free countries to provide their social media history from the past five years. According to Jin10, Massachusetts Senator Edward Markey and Oregon Senator Ron Wyden addressed a letter to U.S. Customs and Border Protection Commissioner Rodney Scott, urging the agency to retract the proposal. The senators argued that the new policy represents a significant invasion of privacy. They expressed concerns that individuals visiting the U.S. for family visits, business with American companies, or events like the upcoming World Cup would be subjected to extensive digital surveillance. They emphasized that ordinary visitors should not have their online activities monitored by the expanding surveillance system of the Trump administration, and choosing to visit the U.S. should not equate to forfeiting personal privacy rights.

data.title: The "BTC 5-Minute Price Fluctuation Market" on Polymarket saw $25.2 million in trading volume over 40 hours.
data.description: Blockworks researcher Kunal Doshi published an article on the X platform stating that Polymarket's newly launched 5-minute BTC market generated $25.2 million in trading volume within 40 hours of its launch. The average trading volume per window was approximately $52,000, reaching 10% to 20% of the 15-minute market's trading volume. Data shows that 7.1% of individual addresses simultaneously bought both Yes and No orders within a single window, while only 4.2% of addresses did so across more than 50 windows. Although these addresses were fewer in number, they accounted for 7.5% of the total trading volume and 23.4% of the total number of transactions. Of these, 92.6% of the orders were buy orders, and 72.3% were pending orders that were filled. These addresses profited in 80% of the windows, with a median Yes + No transaction price of $0.9823. Currently, the market exhibits early arbitrage activity; if structured liquidity is introduced, the 5-minute market's trading volume is expected to surpass that of the 15-minute market.
